define void @test1(ptr nocapture noundef %a, i32 noundef signext %n) {
; CHECK-LABEL: test1:
; CHECK:       # %bb.0: # %entry
; CHECK-NEXT:    blez a1, .LBB0_3
; CHECK-NEXT:  # %bb.1: # %for.body.preheader
; CHECK-NEXT:    slli a1, a1, 2

; CHECK-NEXT:    lw a2, 0(a0)
; CHECK-NEXT:    addi a2, a2, 4
; CHECK-NEXT:    sw a2, 0(a0)
; CHECK-NEXT:    addi a0, a0, 4
; CHECK-NEXT:    bne a0, a1, .LBB0_2
; CHECK-NEXT:  .LBB0_3: # %for.cond.cleanup
; CHECK-NEXT:    ret
entry:
  %cmp3 = icmp sgt i32 %n, 0
  br i1 %cmp3, label %for.body.preheader, label %for.cond.cleanup

for.body.preheader:                               ; preds = %entry
  %wide.trip.count = zext nneg i32 %n to i64
  br label %for.body

for.cond.cleanup:                                 ; preds = %for.body, %entry
  ret void

for.body:                                         ; preds = %for.body, %for.body.preheader
  %indvars.iv = phi i64 [ 0, %for.body.preheader ], [ %indvars.iv.next, %for.body ]
  %arrayidx = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %a, i64 %indvars.iv
  %0 = load i32, ptr %arrayidx, align 4
  %add = add nsw i32 %0, 4
  store i32 %add, ptr %arrayidx, align 4
  %indvars.iv.next = add nuw nsw i64 %indvars.iv, 1
  %exitcond.not = icmp eq i64 %indvars.iv.next, %wide.trip.count
  br i1 %exitcond.not, label %for.cond.cleanup, label %for.body
}

issue.title: LSR dropping nneg flag on zext in loop preheader
issue.body:
When induction variables have been widened, the loop preheader often contains something like `%wide.trip.count = zext nneg %x`. If LSR changes the loop, the nneg attribute appears to get lost.

RISC-V now depends on the nneg flag to form ISD::SIGN_EXTEND in SelectionDAGBuilder instead of converting zext to sext in RISCVCodeGenPrepare::visitZExtInst so the loss of this flag hurts RISC-V.

I'll work on extracting a reproducer.

cc: @preames @nikic
issue.author: topperc

comments.author: preames
comments.body:
Dug into this a bit.  The problem is coming from FindValueInExprValueMap which is trying to reuse an existing expression in ScalarEvolutionExpander.  It is - in general, correctly - stripping the flags from the existing zext to enable sharing with a newly introduced zext.

We can locally prove that the flags are valid on the new zext.  There doesn't seem to be any type of framework in the existing code for this type of reasoning.  I have a locally hacky patch which confirms the diagnosis, but need to think more about the "right" fix.  

One idea I haven't explored would be to simply move the RISCVCodeGenPrepare pass after LSR.  

I'm going to be distracted for the next couple days, if anyone else is interested, feel free to pick this up.

comments.author: preames
comments.body: Should be resolved now that https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/pull/72431 has landed.
